definitions: An economic strategy adopted by a country or entity to withstand external pressures, sanctions, or blockades, often by promoting self-sufficiency, domestic production, and alternative trade routes.
A system designed to minimize vulnerability to foreign economic influence and maximize resilience against hostile economic measures.
examples: The nation implemented a resistance economy to counter the effects of international sanctions.
Developing local industries was a key component of their resistance economy strategy.
The government emphasized a resistance economy to achieve greater economic independence.
